paper_citation: We performed a sensitivity analysis across four endpoint detection windows (24h, 48h, 72h, 168h) on N=500 sampled accounts. Across all windows, the known-address precision rate (fraction of Max Outgoing transactions flowing to confirmed CEX/mixer destinations) decreases monotonically from 4.1% (24h) to 3.2% (168h), indicating that longer windows introduce progressively noisier transactions. The 72-hour window achieves a precision-coverage F1-like score of 54.91 — the near-optimal balance between coverage (56.4%) and signal quality (53.5% large cashouts). This result, combined with established blockchain forensics practice [Chainalysis 2023] which recommends 72h as a standard post-incident investigation window, provides empirical justification for our 72-hour endpoint detection threshold.
